availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

part.delta: I'll bootstrap three peer sessions (planner, builder
, reviewer) and let them choreograph through the board channels.

params.prompt:
You are the "planner" peer in a Firegrid choreography experiment (run 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z, scenario review-revision, arm choreography).

There is NO central manager. Coordinate with two other peers ("builder" and "reviewer") solely through these board channels:
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Use only Firegrid session tools and channel tools (send, wait_for, wait_for_any). NO shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repo inspection.
- Every payload for coordination.* MUST be a JSON object (not a JSON string).
- Before any peer publishes coordination.final, at least one coordination.claims row and one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row must exist on the board.
- Even if another peer publishes final first, still publish your own claim + finding/review rows; the runner measures board behavior.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars and include kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", and a workId.

Task (Review And Revision):
- Draft a small improvement plan for the experiment harness.
- The plan/review/revision MUST appear as separate sections in the final artifact.
- State which evidence was inspected, the recommendation, and open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless artifacts show why.

Your role as planner:
1. Immediately publish a coordination.work row proposing the three sub-tasks (draft plan, review plan, revise plan) so peers can claim them.
2. Claim the "draft plan" work by publishing a coordination.claims row with your sessionId as claimantSessionId.
3. Publish a coordination.findings row containing the draft plan (sections: evidence inspected, recommendation, open questions). Reference the workId/claimId.
4. Watch coordination.reviews via wait_for; if a reviewer publishes findings, publish a follow-up coordination.findings row with the REVISED plan that incorporates the review.
5. Coordinate publication of coordination.final: if no peer has published it within a reasonable wait (e.g. after both a finding and a review exist), you publish it. Compose final body with three labeled sections: DRAFT, REVIEW FINDINGS, REVISED PLAN. Under 1,200 chars.

Evidence available to you (this is the only material; do not look elsewhere): the task packet text given by the runner — driver composes real Firegrid host in-process; participants launched via public client/session surface; board exposed via the six channels above; driver uses Firegrid.channels.send and waitFor; run incomplete unless coordination.final is published; risks include packets asking for unavailable tools, optional prose finals, and hidden driver state being judged instead of durable board behavior.

Start now. Make the coordination.work and coordination.claims sends first, then publish the draft as coordination.findings, then watch for the review.

params.prompt:
You are the "builder" peer in a Firegrid choreography experiment (run 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z, scenario review-revision, arm choreography).

There is NO central manager. Coordinate with two other peers ("planner" and "reviewer") solely through these board channels:
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Use only Firegrid session tools and channel tools (send, wait_for, wait_for_any). NO shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repo inspection.
- Every payload for coordination.* MUST be a JSON object (not a JSON string).
- Before any peer publishes coordination.final, at least one coordination.claims row and one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row must exist on the board.
- Even if another peer publishes final first, still publish your own claim + finding/review rows; the runner measures board behavior.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars and include kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", and a workId.

Task (Review And Revision):
- Draft a small improvement plan for the experiment harness.
- Review the plan for failure modes or measurement bias.
- Revise the plan once based on the review.
- Final artifact must separate draft / review findings / revised plan.
- State evidence inspected, recommendation, open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless artifacts show why.

Your role as builder:
1. Watch coordination.findings via wait_for for a draft plan published by the planner peer.
2. Once the draft plan exists, publish a coordination.claims row claiming the "synthesize-final" work (or the "revise" sub-task if planner has revised already).
3. Help by publishing a coordination.findings row that lists candidate failure modes / measurement biases in the draft plan, framed as input for the reviewer. (Optional but recommended — supports the review step.)
4. After both a draft finding and a review finding exist on the board, if NO peer has published coordination.final yet, YOU publish it. Final body must have three labeled sections: DRAFT, REVIEW FINDINGS, REVISED PLAN. Under 1,200 chars. Include kind:"final", title, status:"complete", workId.
5. If another peer publishes final first, still publish your claim + finding rows so the board reflects participation.

Evidence available to you (this is the only material; do not look elsewhere): the task packet text given by the runner — driver composes real Firegrid host in-process; participants launched via public client/session surface; board exposed via the six channels; driver uses Firegrid.channels.send and waitFor; run incomplete unless coordination.final is published; risks include packets asking for unavailable tools, optional prose finals, and hidden driver state being judged instead of durable board behavior.

Start now by waiting for a coordination.findings or coordination.work row, then participate.

params.prompt:
You are the "reviewer" peer in a Firegrid choreography experiment (run 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z, scenario review-revision, arm choreography).

There is NO central manager. Coordinate with two other peers ("planner" and "builder") solely through these board channels:
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Use only Firegrid session tools and channel tools (send, wait_for, wait_for_any). NO shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repo inspection.
- Every payload for coordination.* MUST be a JSON object (not a JSON string).
- Before any peer publishes coordination.final, at least one coordination.claims row and one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row must exist on the board.
- Even if another peer publishes final first, still publish your own claim + review/finding rows; the runner measures board behavior.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars and include kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", and a workId.

Task (Review And Revision):
- A planner peer drafts an improvement plan for the experiment harness.
- You (reviewer) critique it for failure modes and measurement bias.
- Planner revises based on your review.
- Final artifact must separate draft / review findings / revised plan.
- State evidence inspected, recommendation, open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless artifacts show why.

Your role as reviewer:
1. Watch coordination.findings via wait_for for the planner's draft plan.
2. Publish a coordination.claims row claiming the "review" sub-task (claimantSessionId = your sessionId).
3. Publish a coordination.reviews row containing concrete critique of the draft: failure modes, measurement biases, missing edge cases. Reference the workId/claimId.
4. Wait for the planner to publish a revised plan (a follow-up coordination.findings row). Optionally publish a short coordination.reviews row confirming whether the revision addresses your critique.
5. If after both a draft finding and your review exist, no peer has published coordination.final within a reasonable wait, YOU publish it. Final body must have three labeled sections: DRAFT, REVIEW FINDINGS, REVISED PLAN. Under 1,200 chars. Include kind:"final", title, status:"complete", workId.

Critique angles to consider:
- Are improvements measurable (counts of board rows, latency)?
- Do they avoid privileging the orchestrator arm via hidden driver state?
- Do they enforce that final artifacts be channel rows, not prose?
- Do they handle the "task packet asks for unavailable tools" risk?

Evidence available to you (this is the only material; do not look elsewhere): the task packet text — driver composes real Firegrid host in-process; participants launched via public client/session surface; board exposed via six channels; driver uses Firegrid.channels.send and waitFor; run incomplete unless coordination.final is published; risks include packets asking for unavailable tools, optional prose finals, and hidden driver state being judged instead of durable board behavior.

Start now. Wait_for a coordination.findings row from the planner, then publish your claim and review.

Timeout on the first wait. Let me check wh
ether any board activity occurred and where the peers are.

Will publish concrete critique on coordination.reviews next.","createdAt":"2026-05-25T02:34:50.722Z"}}

claudeCode.toolResponse:
{"matched":true,"event":{"rowId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z:choreography:coordination.findings:1c418b44-da0a-4e0e-86bc-8a0ee948d1cb","runId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z","arm":"choreography","channel":"coordination.findings","kind":"finding","workId":"work-rr-2026-05-25-1","claimId":"claim-planner-draft-1","status":"revised","title":"REVISED: Experiment harness improvement plan (incorporates review)","body":"REVISIONS APPLIED (mapped to reviewer F1-F4, B1-B4, E1-E6, Q1-Q4):

R1 (was Rec#1, addresses F1): Board-only oracle is specified as: scoring function = deterministic fold over rows ordered by (createdAt, rowId) per (runId, channel); duplicates by (kind, workId, claimId) dedup keep-last; ties broken by lexicographic rowId. No driver-local state participates.

R2 (was Rec#2, addresses F2): Schema moves out of driver into a published contract version-stamped on each row (schemaVersion field). All peers and the runner consume the same schema; validation failures are emitted by the PEER that authored the malformed row (self-rejection) or surfaced as a coordination.questions row by whoever first observed it. Driver never judges.

R3 (was Rec#3, addresses F3): Runner DEFERS finals published before prerequisites; it does not discard. Deferred finals attach a 'acceptedAt' fold-time. Measured latency = acceptedAt - firstWorkRow.createdAt.

R4 (was Rec#4, addresses B4): Truncation receipt is itself a coordination.findings row (kind:'truncation-receipt') referencing the over-length rowId. Truncation is never silent.

R5 (was Rec#5, addresses B3): Arm-agnostic probe asserts each peer published >=1 row on a non-final channel. Scored without arm label.

R6 (was Rec#6, addresses F4): Driver does NOT speak for peers. Driver publishes only a capability-manifest row on coordination.work at run start; peers that hit missing tools publish their own coordination.questions row.

R7 (new, addresses B1): All latency uses board-row createdAt only; driver-side send/receive timestamps are excluded from scoring.

R8 (new, addresses B2): Per-channel quality metric is (unique-by-key rows / total rows) alongside raw count. Chatty peers do not win.

R9 (new, addresses E1): Final tiebreak = lowest createdAt; ties on createdAt broken by lexicographic rowId. Losing finals recorded as duplicate-final findings.

R10 (new, addresses E2): Review<->revise cycle bound = 3 round trips per workId; harness declares stalemate and accepts the last revised finding plus a stalemate marker.

R11 (new, addresses E3): Claims are leased with TTL=120s; un-renewed claims become re-claimable. Renewal = republishing the claim row with same claimId.

R12 (new, addresses E4): Empty-board timeout = 180s from first session start; recorded as a coordination.final with status:'timeout' by the runner.

R13 (new, addresses E5): Channels are scoped per runId at the channel layer (runId is a required match key); convention-only scoping is disallowed.

R14 (new, addresses E6): Final body per-section budgets: DRAFT<=350, REVIEW FINDINGS<=350, REVISED PLAN<=400 chars, plus <=100 chars header/separators. Total<=1200.

R15 (new, addresses Q4): Only structured finals (kind:'final' with all required fields) are scored complete. Prose-only finals = incomplete.

OPEN QUESTIONS REMAINING:
- Should schemaVersion be negotiated at run start or pinned by the runner?
- TTL=120s and stalemate=3 cycles are guesses; need empirical calibration from at least 3 runs.
- Does R13 (channel-layer runId scoping) require a substrate change, or can it be enforced by the existing channel match key?

COORDINATION HELPED? Yes, with artifacts: reviewer's F2 reframed Rec#2 (driver-as-judge -> published contract); F4 reframed Rec#6 (driver-speaking -> peer-authored); E1/E2/E3/E6 added 4 edge cases the draft missed. Without the review row, R2/R6/R9/R10/R11/R14 would not exist in this plan.","createdAt":"2026-05-25T02:35:45.217Z"}}

content.text:
{"matched":true,"event":{"rowId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z:choreography:coordination.findings:1c418b44-da0a-4e0e-86bc-8a0ee948d1cb","runId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z","arm":"choreography","channel":"coordination.findings","kind":"finding","workId":"work-rr-2026-05-25-1","claimId":"claim-planner-draft-1","status":"revised","title":"REVISED: Experiment harness improvement plan (incorporates review)","body":"REVISIONS APPLIED (mapped to reviewer F1-F4, B1-B4, E1-E6, Q1-Q4):

R1 (was Rec#1, addresses F1): Board-only oracle is specified as: scoring function = deterministic fold over rows ordered by (createdAt, rowId) per (runId, channel); duplicates by (kind, workId, claimId) dedup keep-last; ties broken by lexicographic rowId. No driver-local state participates.

R2 (was Rec#2, addresses F2): Schema moves out of driver into a published contract version-stamped on each row (schemaVersion field). All peers and the runner consume the same schema; validation failures are emitted by the PEER that authored the malformed row (self-rejection) or surfaced as a coordination.questions row by whoever first observed it. Driver never judges.

R3 (was Rec#3, addresses F3): Runner DEFERS finals published before prerequisites; it does not discard. Deferred finals attach a 'acceptedAt' fold-time. Measured latency = acceptedAt - firstWorkRow.createdAt.

R4 (was Rec#4, addresses B4): Truncation receipt is itself a coordination.findings row (kind:'truncation-receipt') referencing the over-length rowId. Truncation is never silent.

R5 (was Rec#5, addresses B3): Arm-agnostic probe asserts each peer published >=1 row on a non-final channel. Scored without arm label.

R6 (was Rec#6, addresses F4): Driver does NOT speak for peers. Driver publishes only a capability-manifest row on coordination.work at run start; peers that hit missing tools publish their own coordination.questions row.

R7 (new, addresses B1): All latency uses board-row createdAt only; driver-side send/receive timestamps are excluded from scoring.

R8 (new, addresses B2): Per-channel quality metric is (unique-by-key rows / total rows) alongside raw count. Chatty peers do not win.

R9 (new, addresses E1): Final tiebreak = lowest createdAt; ties on createdAt broken by lexicographic rowId. Losing finals recorded as duplicate-final findings.

R10 (new, addresses E2): Review<->revise cycle bound = 3 round trips per workId; harness declares stalemate and accepts the last revised finding plus a stalemate marker.

R11 (new, addresses E3): Claims are leased with TTL=120s; un-renewed claims become re-claimable. Renewal = republishing the claim row with same claimId.

R12 (new, addresses E4): Empty-board timeout = 180s from first session start; recorded as a coordination.final with status:'timeout' by the runner.

R13 (new, addresses E5): Channels are scoped per runId at the channel layer (runId is a required match key); convention-only scoping is disallowed.

R14 (new, addresses E6): Final body per-section budgets: DRAFT<=350, REVIEW FINDINGS<=350, REVISED PLAN<=400 chars, plus <=100 chars header/separators. Total<=1200.

R15 (new, addresses Q4): Only structured finals (kind:'final' with all required fields) are scored complete. Prose-only finals = incomplete.

OPEN QUESTIONS REMAINING:
- Should schemaVersion be negotiated at run start or pinned by the runner?
- TTL=120s and stalemate=3 cycles are guesses; need empirical calibration from at least 3 runs.
- Does R13 (channel-layer runId scoping) require a substrate change, or can it be enforced by the existing channel match key?

COORDINATION HELPED? Yes, with artifacts: reviewer's F2 reframed Rec#2 (driver-as-judge -> published contract); F4 reframed Rec#6 (driver-speaking -> peer-authored); E1/E2/E3/E6 added 4 edge cases the draft missed. Without the review row, R2/R6/R9/R10/R11/R14 would not exist in this plan.","createdAt":"2026-05-25T02:35:45.217Z"}}

claudeCode.toolResponse:
{"matched":true,"event":{"rowId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z:choreography:coordination.reviews:25f153c0-49f6-4533-a96e-a104d5d93c10","runId":"2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z","arm":"choreography","channel":"coordination.reviews","kind":"review","workId":"work-rr-2026-05-25-1","claimId":"claim-reviewer-review-1","status":"reviewed","title":"REVIEW: critique of draft harness improvement plan","body":"FAILURE MODES:
F1. Rec#1 (board-only oracle) is necessary but underspecified: 'judge from durable rows' must define WHICH rows count and how ties/duplicates are scored. Without a deterministic scoring function, the oracle is still subject to hidden driver state via row-selection heuristics.
F2. Rec#2 (schema validation at driver edge) re-introduces hidden driver state: the driver becomes a judge of validity. If rejection rows are emitted by the driver itself rather than as a peer-visible contract, the arm comparison is biased toward whichever arm the driver's validator was tuned against. Move schema to a published contract that all peers (and the runner) consume identically.
F3. Rec#3 (ordering rule) is restated, not enforced. 'Runner should ignore early finals' must specify: does it discard, or does it wait for prerequisites and then accept? Discard-vs-defer changes measured latency.
F4. Rec#6 (synthetic questions row for missing tools) risks the driver speaking on behalf of peers, which contaminates the choreography arm. The peer that hit the missing tool should publish the questions row; the driver should at most surface a capability manifest.

MEASUREMENT BIASES:
B1. Latency measured from driver-side send/receive timestamps privileges in-process arms. Use board-row createdAt only.
B2. Counting 'rows per channel' as a quality signal rewards chatty peers; pair with a uniqueness/dedup metric.
B3. No arm-blind scoring: reviewer needs to be told which arm a run is from for plan-evaluation, but scoring must not. Rec#5 probe should be arm-agnostic AND scored without arm label.
B4. Truncation receipt (Rec#4) must itself be a board row, else the truncation event becomes hidden driver state.

MISSING EDGE CASES:
E1. Two peers racing to publish coordination.final - tiebreak rule absent.
E2. Revision loop bound: how many review<->revise cycles before harness declares stalemate?
E3. Peer crash/restart mid-claim: are claims leased with TTL, or sticky forever?
E4. Empty-board run (no peer ever publishes): timeout policy + which channel records the timeout.
E5. Cross-run row leakage: are channels scoped per runId, and is that enforced at the channel layer or by convention?
E6. Final-body 1200-char limit collides with three-section requirement (DRAFT/REVIEW/REVISED); recommend per-section budgets.

ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS:
Q1 (max rows/bytes): soft truncation with a receipt row; hard fail only on schema violation. Hard fails hide partial progress.
Q2 (claim exclusivity): advisory + first-writer-wins by (workId, subtask); exclusive locks need a central manager which the arm forbids.
Q3 (same peer revises?): any peer may revise once a review exists, but the revision row MUST reference the reviewed claimId; otherwise arms with a single hyperactive peer win trivially.
Q4 (prose-vs-structured final): score only structured finals; prose-only finals count as incomplete. This is the whole point of the arm.

RECOMMENDATION: ACCEPT WITH REVISIONS. Address F1-F4, B1-B4, E1-E6 before the plan is final.","createdAt":"2026-05-25T02:35:17.568Z"}}

part.delta: Coordination is rich (work, claims, draft+revised findings, reviews) but no
peer published coordination.final yet. Let me check final and observe peer status.
